whatThisIs: Decode the cryptic strings on a U.S. health insurance card into a printable, plain-English summary - the member ID format, the group number, the BIN and PCN that route pharmacy claims, the payer/RxBIN telephone numbers, the in-network customer-service line, and the deductible/copay/coinsurance fields when they are printed. Sophie Well lays them out in the order a front-desk registrar, a pharmacy tech, or a patient calling the payer actually needs them.
whenToUse: Use this when registering a new patient and the card layout is unfamiliar, when a pharmacy rejects a prescription with "missing BIN" and you cannot find it on the physical card, or when a patient wants a printable reference they can keep with the card itself instead of squinting at fine print at every visit. The output is reference only - it does not verify eligibility or benefits, which still requires a payer call or an electronic 270/271 transaction - but it gets the numbers off the card and into a legible format in one pass.
